title: A Fight for the Soul of One of Australia’s Biggest Unions
summary: Union density in Australia has been in free fall for decades. In 1992, nearly 40 percent of workers were union members; today, that figure hovers around 12.5 percent for public sector unions. In the private sector it is closer to 8 percent. Strikes and industrial action have collapsed even more dramatically. Militancy has not simply […]

title: Is Palantir Under Contract to Surveil the Federal Workforce?
summary: Palantir, the Donald Trump–connected spy-tech and AI firm, just scored a no-bid government contract potentially worth millions to help the Agriculture Department implement the White House’s divisive return-to-office directive. Under the guise of national security, the highly “sensitive” tasks to be handled by the billion-dollar tech behemoth will include “employee seat assignments” and “space utilization.” Despite […]

title: The Labor Movement Must Go All In on Organizing Amazon
summary: There’s only one time in American history to look to if we’re seeking a rapid and lasting revitalization of unions, and that’s the rise of the Congress of Industrial Organizations (CIO) in the mid-1930s. There were many things that made that particular moment successful, but one of the key ones was that they took aim […]
reason: The piece directly engages the routing question of how labor power gets built structurally, using the CIO precedent to argue for organizing at the node that controls the largest logistics and warehouse network in the country.
angle: Use the routing architecture tool: Amazon is not just a workplace but an infrastructural chokepoint, and organizing it represents a flow-level change in how labor power moves through the economy, not merely a representational win.

title: The Iraq War Was Not About Oil
summary: In the wake of a full-scale assault on Iran by the United States and Israel, and the kidnapping of Venezuelan president Nicolás Maduro, oil-based theories of imperialism are understandably getting attention. title: The Toxic Finance Behind Europe’s Plans for Ukraine
summary: When the euro crisis hit in early 2010, with Greece the first domino to fall, all it took to surmise that Europe had no intention of resolving the euro crisis was one good look at the toxic finance the EU concocted in response. title: America’s Next Generation of Socialist Organizers
summary: This year’s Organizing Conference of the Young Democratic Socialists of America (YDSA), which Jacobin cosponsored, quickly sold out, drawing more than four hundred young socialists to the “Rooted in Struggle” gathering — a sign of how far the organization has grown since its humble beginnings as the youth section of the Democratic Socialist Organizing Committee. […]
reason: This is primarily representational coverage of an organization's growth and conference attendance, not an analysis of routing-level change or concrete prefigurative institution-building.

title: Humanity Has Altered an Asteroid’s Orbit Around the Sun
summary: A NASA spacecraft into a small asteroid in 2022 moved its orbit around the Sun, according to a study that presents the “first-ever measurement of human-caused change in the heliocentric orbit of a celestial body.”
reason: This is a scientific milestone story with no meaningful connection to the framework's core concerns about capture, enclosure, commons, or resistance.

title: California colleges spend millions on faulty AI systems: 'The chatbot is outdated’
summary: Community colleges are spending millions on AI-powered chatbots that students say often give inaccurate answers. Many might see upgrades this year.
reason: This story shows epistemic incompetence of centralization in action: institutions replacing distributed human advising knowledge with centralized AI systems that systematically destroy the local, relational intelligence they were meant to substitute.
angle: Use the 'epistemic incompetence of centralization' tool to show how AI chatbot procurement routes student needs through a product layer that cannot access the distributed, context-specific knowledge that human advisors held — and how the cost of that failure falls on the most resource-constrained students.

title: Monday’s Headlines Are Rockin’ the Casbah
summary: The king called up his jet fighters, said "you better earn your pay." But now Sharif don't like $100-a-barrel oil prices.

title: Deportation is a Transportation Issue
summary: The shared infrastructure of deportation and transportation highlight an ethical dilemma; can we solve it?
